List
- - American Civil War
- [Battle of Seven Pines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Seven_Pines "Battle of Seven Pines")
- [Battle of Savage's Station](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Savage%27s_Station "Battle of Savage's Station")
- [Battle of Malvern Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Malvern_Hill "Battle of Malvern Hill")
- [Battle of Harpers Ferry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Harpers_Ferry "Battle of Harpers Ferry")
- [Battle of Antietam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Antietam "Battle of Antietam")
- [Battle of Fredericksburg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Fredericksburg "Battle of Fredericksburg") ( [WIA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wounded_in_action "Wounded in action"))
- [Battle of Gettysburg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Gettysburg "Battle of Gettysburg")
- [Battle of Chickamauga](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Chickamauga "Battle of Chickamauga")
- [Battle of the Wilderness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_the_Wilderness "Battle of the Wilderness") [**†**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Killed_in_action "Killed in action") | **James Drayton Nance** (1837-1864) was a Confederate Colonel of the [American Civil War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War"). He was known for commanding the [3rd South Carolina Infantry Regiment](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=3rd_South_Carolina_Infantry_Regiment&action=edit&redlink=1 "3rd South Carolina Infantry Regiment (page does not exist)") throughout the war, primarily serving in the [Peninsula campaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peninsula_campaign "Peninsula campaign"), the [Maryland campaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maryland_campaign "Maryland campaign") and the [Gettysburg campaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gettysburg_campaign "Gettysburg campaign") but was killed at the [Battle of the Wilderness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_the_Wilderness "Battle of the Wilderness"). ## Early life \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=James_D._Nance&action=edit§ion=1 "Edit section: Early life")\] Nance was born on October 10, 1837, at [Newberry, South Carolina](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Newberry,_South_Carolina "Newberry, South Carolina"), as the son of Drayton and Lucy ( [née](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/N%C3%A9e "Née") Williams) Nance.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-Kershaw-1) His maternal great grandfather, [James Williams](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_Williams_(Colonel) "James Williams (Colonel)") was a commander of the [Battle of Kings Mountain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Kings_Mountain "Battle of Kings Mountain") and was killed in the battle.[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-South_Carolina-2) During his childhood, Nance was attributed as being extremely truthful and obedient to anyone in a higher authority. Despite this however, he wasn't a pushover as he frequently asserted his rights among his classmates and manifested a spirit of justice and fairness.[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-FOOTNOTECarwile1890249-3) Before the American Civil War, he graduated from the Citadel Military Academy of Charleston and was practicing law in 1859.[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-FOOTNOTECarwile1890249-50-4)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-Tiger_Prints-5) Around the same time, Nance was elected to be the Captain of the Quitman Rifles which was a military formation originating from Newberry.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-Kershaw-1) He was also a practicing Baptist Christian, joining since the age of 17.[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-FOOTNOTECarwile1890250-6)[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-South_Carolina-2) ## American Civil War \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=James_D._Nance&action=edit§ion=2 "Edit section: American Civil War")\] After the [American Civil War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War") broke out, Nance enlisted as the Captain of Company E within the [3rd South Carolina Infantry Regiment](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=3rd_South_Carolina_Infantry_Regiment&action=edit&redlink=1 "3rd South Carolina Infantry Regiment (page does not exist)") on April 14, 1861, as the Quitman Rifles was incorporated within the Regiment and briefly saw service at the [First Battle of Bull Run](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First_Battle_of_Bull_Run "First Battle of Bull Run").[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-Antietam-7) He was promoted to Colonel on May 16, 1862.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-Colonels-8) During his service within [Joseph B. Kershaw](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_B._Kershaw "Joseph B. Kershaw")'s brigade, he was described as a thorough tactician and disciplinarian, notably earning the respect from General [James Conner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_Conner_(general) "James Conner (general)").[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-Kershaw-1) Nance saw his first notable action at the [Peninsula campaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peninsula_campaign "Peninsula campaign"), taking part in the battles of [Seven Pines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Seven_Pines "Battle of Seven Pines"), [Savage's Station](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Savage%27s_Station "Battle of Savage's Station") and [Malvern Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Malvern_Hill "Battle of Malvern Hill"). He was then transferred over to the [Maryland campaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maryland_campaign "Maryland campaign") to participate in the [Battle of Harpers Ferry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Harpers_Ferry "Battle of Harpers Ferry") and later at the bigger [Battle of Antietam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Antietam "Battle of Antietam"). After getting wounded at the [Battle of Fredericksburg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Fredericksburg "Battle of Fredericksburg"), he later returned to participate at the [Battle of Gettysburg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Gettysburg "Battle of Gettysburg") and the [Battle of Chickamauga](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Chickamauga "Battle of Chickamauga"). During the war, Nance was described as being "dauntless but discreet, buoyant and hopeful, yet fully alive to the stern duties of life: with a clear head and great self-possession, he was in every way reliable."[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-FOOTNOTECarwile1890251-9) Nance was killed during the [Battle of the Wilderness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_the_Wilderness "Battle of the Wilderness") as [William D. Rutherford](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/William_D._Rutherford "William D. Rutherford") reported by telegram "that Col. Nance fell in battle today - - pierced by five balls."[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-Antietam-7)[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-Colonels-8) At the time of his death, his superior officers were already prepared for his promotion to [Brigadier general](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brigadier_general_(Confederate_States_Army) "Brigadier general (Confederate States Army)").[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-South_Carolina-2)[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_note-Kershaw-1) ## References \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=James_D._Nance&action=edit§ion=3 "Edit section: References")\] 1. ^ [Jump up to: _**a**_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_ref-Kershaw_1-0) [_**b**_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_ref-Kershaw_1-1) [_**c**_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_ref-Kershaw_1-2) [_**d**_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_D._Nance#cite_ref-Kershaw_1-3)Dickert, David Augustus (1899).
